news.headline: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over 'deceptive' GLP-1 advertising campaign
news.summary: Novo Nordisk filed suit in the US District Court for the District of New Jersey on July 21, 2026, accusing Eli Lilly of running a nationwide ad campaign (over 700 million impressions since late April) that compares Zepbound's and Mounjaro's highest doses against an outdated, lower dose of Wegovy to make Lilly's drugs look more effective. Novo is seeking a permanent injunction and corrective advertising; Lilly says its ads are truthful and evidence-based.

news.headline: European Commission approves Wegovy pill as first oral GLP-1 for weight management in the EU
news.summary: The European Commission approved oral semaglutide 25 mg (Wegovy pill) for adults with obesity or overweight with a weight-related comorbidity across all 27 EU member states plus Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein, following the CHMP's May 2026 positive opinion; a single ready-to-use pen for the higher 7.2 mg injectable dose was also cleared. Eli Lilly still has no approved oral GLP-1 rival in Europe, giving Novo a first-mover window in the region.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk's denecimig (Mim8) shows positive long-term safety and efficacy in phase 3 hemophilia A extension study
news.summary: At the ISTH Congress in Paris, Novo Nordisk presented interim FRONTIER4 extension-study data on 426 hemophilia A patients showing low annualized bleeding rates of 0.75 in adults/adolescents and 0.37 in children with a clean safety profile across weekly, biweekly and monthly dosing. The data support the BLA already filed with the FDA in September 2025 and reinforce Novo's pipeline diversification beyond GLP-1 obesity/diabetes drugs.

news.headline: Vivani Medical partners with Novo Nordisk to evaluate twice-yearly semaglutide implant NPM-139
news.summary: Novo Nordisk signed a non-exclusive agreement with Vivani Medical to evaluate NPM-139, a miniature twice-yearly semaglutide implant built on Vivani's NanoPortal platform, as it broadens obesity-drug delivery options beyond injectables and pills. Vivani expects to start a first-in-human Phase 1 study against Wegovy injections in mid-2026.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk restructures Korea operations as Eli Lilly's Zepbound/Mounjaro extends lead over Wegovy/Ozempic
news.summary: Novo Nordisk Korea merged its obesity and diabetes commercial units under a locally experienced executive to speed decision-making, as US weekly Zepbound prescriptions pulled more than 100,000 ahead of Wegovy (near 60% share) and Korean Mounjaro sales roughly tripled Wegovy's despite a Novo wholesale price cut of about 40%. The move underscores the commercial ground Novo needs to recover from Lilly.

news.headline: Medicare GLP-1 Bridge cuts Wegovy cost to $50/month copay for eligible beneficiaries
news.summary: Starting July 1, 2026, the CMS-run Medicare GLP-1 Bridge demonstration gives eligible Medicare Part D beneficiaries with obesity access to Wegovy injection and tablets (alongside Eli Lilly's Zepbound and Foundayo) for a flat $50 monthly copay, running through the end of 2027. The program removes a major reimbursement barrier that had largely limited obesity-drug uptake to commercially insured and cash-pay patients.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk discloses cyberattack and data breach involving clinical trial and IP data
news.summary: Novo Nordisk disclosed on June 11, 2026 that hackers had accessed internal IT systems; extortion group FulcrumSec claims it spent over two months inside the network and stole roughly 1.3 TB of data, including source code, clinical trial information, pseudonymized patient data, employee/HCP records and AI model details, after Novo refused a $25 million ransom demand. The group has since begun leaking portions of the data (about 264 GB released as of its claims) and is seeking a private buyer for the rest.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: Novo Nordisk is scheduled to report second-quarter and first-half 2026 financial results before market open on August 5, 2026 (07:30 CEST), with investors watching updated full-year guidance (already pointing to a low-single-digit sales decline for 2026), US Wegovy/Ozempic volume and share trends including early Medicare GLP-1 Bridge uptake, and commentary on competitive pressure from Eli Lilly plus the newly filed advertising lawsuit and the June data breach.

catalysts.when: Q4 2026 (exact PDUFA date not yet public)
catalysts.label: FDA decision on CagriSema for chronic weight management
catalysts.detail: CagriSema (cagrilintide/semaglutide) NDA was submitted in December 2025 based on REDEFINE 1/2 data under standard (non-priority) review, implying a decision around late 2026. Approval would give Novo a next-generation obesity drug to compete with Lilly's Zepbound/orforglipron franchise, though the head-to-head REDEFINE 4 study showed CagriSema fell short of noninferiority versus tirzepatide 15 mg on weight loss (23.0% vs 25.5% at 84 weeks); positive REIMAGINE 2 type 2 diabetes data (1.91-point HbA1c reduction and 14.2% weight loss vs Ozempic's 1.76 points/10.2%) support a possible diabetes label expansion filing as well.

catalysts.when: H2 2026 (approx., ~10-12 months after Sept 2025 BLA submission)
catalysts.label: FDA decision on denecimig (Mim8) for hemophilia A
catalysts.detail: Denecimig's BLA was submitted to the FDA in September 2025, supported by fresh FRONTIER4 long-term extension data (annualized bleeding rates of 0.75 in adults/adolescents and 0.37 in children) presented at ISTH in Paris on July 11, 2026. Approval would give Novo a new once-weekly-to-once-monthly FVIIIa-mimetic prophylaxis option in hemophilia A, diversifying revenue beyond GLP-1s.

catalysts.label: Wegovy pill commercial roll-out across EU member states
catalysts.detail: Following the European Commission's July 15, 2026 marketing authorization, Novo Nordisk plans to commercially launch the oral Wegovy pill across EU countries through H2 2026, with pricing and reimbursement negotiated market-by-market. UK availability via private prescription began after the MHRA's June 11, 2026 approval, while NHS reimbursement of the pill still awaits a separate NICE review.

catalysts.label: First-in-human Phase 1 trial of NPM-139 semaglutide implant (Vivani partnership)
catalysts.detail: Following the July 7, 2026 agreement between Novo Nordisk and Vivani Medical, Vivani expects to start a randomized, first-in-human Phase 1 study of NPM-139 -- a twice-yearly semaglutide implant using its NanoPortal platform, with Wegovy injections as an active comparator -- aimed at supporting a subsequent Phase 2 dose-ranging study.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on Ozempic 25 mg tablet supplemental NDA for type 2 diabetes
catalysts.detail: Novo Nordisk filed a supplemental drug application for an additional 25 mg strength of Ozempic tablets (oral semaglutide) for type 2 diabetes, adding to the already-approved 1.5 mg, 4 mg and 9 mg doses on the market since the Ozempic pill's May 2026 US launch; the FDA decision is expected by year-end 2026.

catalysts.label: US list-price cuts for Wegovy, Ozempic and Rybelsus take effect
catalysts.detail: Announced February 24, 2026: Novo will lower US list prices to $675/month for Wegovy injection/tablets and Ozempic injection/tablets (roughly 50% and 35% cuts respectively) starting January 1, 2027, while leaving direct-to-consumer self-pay pricing unchanged; a factor in future US revenue-per-script trends, though partly offset by the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge's $50 copay driving volume from mid-2026.

catalysts.label: DKK 15 billion share buyback programme
catalysts.detail: Novo Nordisk is executing a share repurchase programme of up to DKK 15 billion over a 12-month period that began February 4, 2026 (current tranche running May 6, 2026 to February 1, 2027 for up to DKK 11.2 billion); weekly updates through July 20, 2026 confirmed cumulative repurchases since inception of roughly 25.0 million B shares (average price ~DKK 274.58, transaction value ~DKK 6.85 billion), with further weekly updates due through the programme's conclusion.

pulse: Novo Nordisk keeps notching regulatory and pipeline wins -- EU-wide approval of the Wegovy pill (July 15), positive long-term hemophilia A data for denecimig at ISTH (July 11), a Vivani Medical partnership exploring a twice-yearly semaglutide implant (July 7), and the July 1 Medicare GLP-1 Bridge cutting US patient costs to $50/month -- but Eli Lilly's Zepbound/Mounjaro franchise keeps outselling Wegovy/Ozempic in the US and Korea, prompting a defensive restructuring of Novo's Korea operations (July 6), a July 21 lawsuit accusing Lilly of deceptive comparative ads, and lingering fallout from a June 11 cyberattack that exposed clinical trial and IP data after Novo refused a $25 million ransom. For the long-dated call position, the key swing factors are whether pipeline and access momentum can offset Lilly's commercial lead, with the August 5, 2026 Q2 earnings report (against a full-year guide already pointing to a low-single-digit sales decline) and CagriSema's expected Q4 2026 FDA decision as the next major tests.
